summary: Written days before the 2016 US presidential election, DeVault expresses deep disillusionment with American politics, describing both Clinton and Trump as unacceptable candidates. He broadens his critique to systemic corruption in Congress, citing lobbying, climate inaction, the wealth gap, mass surveillance, the war on drugs, and failures of the justice system. Having recently left his job at Linode, he reveals he is seriously considering moving abroad for the first time. He concludes that America has become an oligarchy ruled by money, despite his genuine love for the country and its people.
key_points: Both major 2016 presidential candidates are presented as deeply flawed — Clinton for corruption and Trump for racism and incompetence
Congressional corruption driven by lobbyist money undermines action on climate change, healthcare, infrastructure, and wealth inequality
Mass surveillance, the war on drugs, private prisons, and erosion of constitutional rights represent systemic failures of American governance
America has functionally become an oligarchy rather than a representative democracy
Despite loving America's people and land, DeVault is seriously considering leaving the country
money_quotes: The next president of the United States will probably be Hillary Clinton. I'm sure I don't have to tell you how ridiculous this is.
We're facing climate change and our politicians are taking money from rich fossil fuel lobbyists and damning our species to extinction.
We could provide free healthcare for all Americans too, if it wasn't for the ongoing debate about whether or not being alive and healthy is a fundamental human right.
Mass surveillance remains in full effect even years after Snowden's revelations.
There's a word for what America is: corrupt.
We aren't a representative democracy by any stretch of the imagination. We have become an oligarchy. We are ruled by money.
I love America, honestly. My whole family is here and I connect most with the American people.
I don't expect anywhere else to be perfect, but we should be ashamed of how we look next to some other countries out there.
key_insight: America's systemic corruption — from elections to prisons to surveillance — has transformed it from a representative democracy into an oligarchy, making emigration a rational response for those who still believe in democratic ideals.
